MEMORANDUM OF COSTS AND DISBURSEMENTS, $1,757.01 TOTAL COSTS, MATURE DATE NOV-29-2007, FILED BY DEFENDANT BRITO, BRISLEY

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BRISLEY BRITO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM IS BARRED BY WORKERS COMPENSATION; WHICH IS TO BE LIBERALLY CONSTRUED IN FAVOR OF AWARDING COVERAGE FOR EMPLOYEES. ARRIAGA V COUNTY OF ALAMEDA (1995) 9 CAL. 4TH 1055, 1065. IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT PLAINTIFF REGULARLY SOUGHT EXTRA SHIFTS, THAT IS WHY HE WAS ON THE PREMISES AND SO THE INJURY OCCURRED IN THE COURSE AND SCOPE OF HIS WORK: GARDNER V INDUSTRIAL ACC. COMM. (1946) 73 CAL APP 2D, 361, 362 THROUGH 67, PRICE V WORKER'S COMPENSATION APPEALS BOARD (1984) 37 CAL. 3D 559. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
